checks.finding: T-001 through T-003 are correctly medium for observable specification-generation behavior, T-004 through T-007 are correctly high for filesystem trust, review-integrity, and authorization surfaces, and T-008 is correctly low for synchronized metadata-only changes; each workflow matches the risk policy.

Every high-risk task, T-004 through T-007, specifies TDD Red-to-Green evidence paths, concrete failing and passing fixtures, independent-review evidence, requirement/acceptance mappings, and rollback verification suitable for downstream gates.

Each task is bounded around one cohesive deliverable: layer templates, cross-layer indexes, interview guidance, feature validation, implementation-review binding, task-review binding, approval-boundary regression, or release metadata.

The task plan assigns concrete coverage for malformed templates, preserved existing files, unaffected layers, absent mockups, LITE exclusion, invalid and traversal-like selectors, legacy compatibility, missing/substituted/tampered inputs, malformed traceability, and invalid or expired approval tokens.

Static integration tests cover document templates and guidance; runtime integration, parity, and regression tests cover Bash/PowerShell behavior; boundary and governance tests cover review integrity and approval eligibility.
All eight tasks include scoped rollback instructions; the high-risk tasks additionally require isolated rollback fixtures that restore baseline hashes or behavior and verify the restored path.

Task scopes and out-of-scope declarations separate layer content, indexes, interview guidance, structure checking, implementation review, task review, approval governance, and release metadata; shared workflow-state infrastructure is divided by traceability versus approval behavior.

Dependencies serialize every meaningful overlap: index and guidance work follow layer artifacts, task-review binding follows implementation-review binding, approval-boundary work follows task-review binding, and release publication follows the functional chain; T-004 is safely independent.
No task is classified as a bugfix or debugging task; this feature adds and extends workflow capabilities, so the calibration's bugfix diagnostic-path condition does not apply.
